zh.Stourton, Wiltshire, England: 英國英格蘭維爾爾斯圖頓
zh.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset於1252年出生在英國爾斯托雷頓。他於大約1272年與Mrs. James Kawksket在英國英格蘭維爾爾斯圖頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1275年在他的家鄉享年23歲。

zh-hans.Stourton, Wiltshire, England: 英国英格兰維爾爾斯圖頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset于1252年出生在英国爾斯托雷頓。他于大约1272年与Mrs. James Kawksket在英国英格兰維爾爾斯圖頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1275年在他的家乡享年23岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hawkset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: nickname from Middle English haukeseye ‘hawk’s eye’.
2 English: the Manchester surname Hawksey preserves an old form of Hawkshaw , recorded as Hawkesey in 1527 ( Lancs Place-Names , p. 63).

variants.name: Hawkshaw
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Hawkshaw in Tottington (Lancashire), named from Old English hafoc ‘hawk’ + sceaga ‘small wood, copse’.

variants.name: Lancs Place-Names
meanings: English: locative name of uncertain origin; apparently from an unidentified place named with Middle English cou ‘cow’ (as a plural cous) + hil(le) ‘hill’. Cowhill in Rishton (Lancs) shows no forms with medial -s- (see Lancs Place-Names , p. 73), and there are no examples of the surname to connect it with Cowshill in Weardale (Durham) or Cows Hill in Hewelsfield (Gloucs).

variants.name: Hawkey
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant, with loss of the medial genitive s, of Hawksey, which is a nickname from Middle English haukeseye ‘hawk's eye’ and also a variant of Hawkshaw , recorded as Hawkesey in 1527.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset was born in 1252, in Storeton, Cheshire, England. He married Mrs. James Kawksket about 1272, in Stourton, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1275, in his hometown, at the age of 23.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset est né en 1252, à Storeton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs. James Kawksket environ 1272, à Stourton, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1275, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 23 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset wurde 1252 in Storeton, Cheshire, England geboren. Er hat um 1272 in Stourton, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Mrs. James Kawksket geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1275 im Alter von 23 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset è nato il nel 1252 nel Storeton, Cheshire, England. Lui sposato Mrs. James Kawksket circa 1272, nel Stourton, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1275, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 23 anni.

ja.Stourton, Wiltshire, England: イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアストウルトン
ja.Storeton, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアストーレトン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawksetは1252年にイギリスチェシュアイアストーレトンで生まれた。約1272年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアストウルトンでMrs. James Kawksketと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1275年に故郷で23歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset(은)는 1252년 영국 체실르 스토레턴에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1272년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 스토우르턴에서 Mrs. James Kawksket(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1275년 그의 고향에서 23세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset nasceu em 1252, em Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mrs. James Kawksket aproximadamente 1272, em Stourton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1275, em sua cidade natal, com 23 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset родился в 1252 г. в местности сторетон, чешир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. James Kawksket около 1272 г. в местности стоуртон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1275 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 23 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir James Hawkset nació en 1252, en Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Mrs. James Kawksket aproximadamente 1272, en Stourton, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1275, en su pueblo, a la edad de 23 años.
